response:
Short answer: because APIM/Fleet expects and is already wired to the Rackspace identity (X‑Auth‑Token) flow — reusing the existing Rackspace identity code was simpler and more reliable than introducing a new Entra client‑credential path.

Key reasons:
- APIM authorization and RBAC are implemented around the Rackspace identity token (X‑Auth‑Token) and the APIM policies map/translate those caller attributes (racker vs customer, named‑value groups, access levels). Using the Rackspace token fits the existing policy model.
- We already had a working Rackspace identity flow (RACKSPACE_USERNAME/RACKSPACE_PASSWORD + shared.janus_helpers.get_rackspace_identity_token). Reusing it avoids new Entra app/client‑secret configuration, audience/JWT validation pitfalls, and extra secret/rotation handling.
- The Entra client‑credential approach introduced configuration/audience/validation fragility (APIM/backend JWT audience and tenant settings) in practice. Switching back eliminated those deployment/auth failures.
- Practical caveat: Rackspace identity tokens do not carry x‑tenant‑id/DDI, so we still supply tenant context from platform data (customer_subscriptions.ddi) per the established guidance.

Result: simpler, more robust auth for the targeted Event Grid ensure endpoint by aligning with how APIM expects operator calls.
context_injected:
--- Episode 1 [codex 2026-04-09] (similarity: 0.6902) ---
azr-compliance-v3 Fleet Event Grid remediation token acquisition was revised again on 2026-04-09: instead of Entra client credentials, backend-py now reuses the existing Rackspace Identity flow backed by RACKSPACE_USERNAME and RACKSPACE_PASSWORD. shared/fleet_event_grid_helpers.py calls shared.janus_helpers.get_rackspace_identity_token(auth_mode='identity') and sends that token as Authorization: Bearer to APIM for the targeted Event Grid ensure endpoint.

--- Episode 2 [codex 2026-04-08] (similarity: 0.5534) ---
On 2026-04-08 in azr-compliance-v3, implemented Event Grid inventory scoping and Rackspace ownership filtering. SyncEventGrid no longer scopes by customer_subscriptions + monitoring service blocks alone; it now scopes to managed monitoring subscriptions by joining customer_subscriptions to lighthouse_delegations, requiring managed_by_tenant_id to equal the Rackspace Lighthouse tenant and delegation_state in active/succeeded. The sync still uses the single Rackspace Lighthouse credential and intentionally does not acquire per-customer tenant tokens. Event Grid rows are now filtered before upsert by a shared ownership classifier: event subscription name must match Rackspace naming conventions (rax-/rackspace-) and destination must match Rackspace-managed webhook/resource patterns (rackspace.com, compliance-v3 azurewebsites hosts, or known azr-fleet/compliance resource IDs). Sync stats now distinguish skipped unmanaged subscriptions, stale/missing subscriptions from SubscriptionNotFound warnings, and filtered unmanaged deployments. GetEventGridDeployments summary and list queries now use the same managed monitoring scope so page KPIs reflect only Rackspace-managed monitoring subscriptions and hide old out-of-scope inventory.

--- Episode 3 [codex 2026-03-19] (similarity: 0.5308) ---
In azr-compliance-v3, X-Auth-Token authorization is defined in APIM, not OpenAPI. APIM validates the Rackspace token, classifies the caller as racker or customer, maps racker named-value groups to MSP-Admin/MSP-Engineer/MSP-Viewer, maps customer roles to admin/billing/observer, enforces route/category RBAC in api-policy.xml, then forwards trusted headers like X-User-Type, X-Auth-Method, X-Access-Level, X-User-Roles, and X-DDI to the backend. The actual racker group display-name values are environment-configured APIM named values, not stored in repo.

--- Episode 4 [codex 2026-03-23] (similarity: 0.513) ---
Rackspace Identity token mint responses do not return x-tenant-id/DDI for operator/Racker accounts. The identity response is only a token mint/lookup payload; x-tenant-id must be supplied from platform context such as customer_subscriptions.ddi or another authoritative customer mapping, not inferred from the minted token response. For Fleet Platform operator API calls that use x-auth-token, do not expect the mint response to provide tenant context.

--- Episode 5 [codex 2026-04-09] (similarity: 0.5049) ---
azr-compliance-v3 Event Grid remediation was switched from a browser-side Fleet API call to a backend-mediated flow because Fleet requires application permission. The backend now acquires a ClientSecretCredential token for scope api://daf679b9-9134-42a1-9998-9cfac279747f/.default and calls https://apim.mscloud.rackspace.com/v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription, polling the returned statusUrl until completion. The frontend calls POST /api/event-grid/remediation/ensure instead of Fleet directly.

--- Episode 6 [codex 2026-04-02] (similarity: 0.4905) ---
On 2026-04-02 in /Users/benj8956/Documents/RackspaceClaw, added the first actual Entra-aware proxy implementation assets for the RackspaceClaw POC. Added runtime-config/proxy/docker-compose.proxy.yml, oauth2-proxy.cfg.template, default.conf.template, proxy.env.example, TLS cert placeholder docs, and docs/entra-proxy-poc.md. The proxy stack uses nginx in front of oauth2-proxy with provider=entra-id and passes X-Auth-Request-Email to the upstream, matching OpenClaw trusted-proxy guidance. Also updated scripts/serve_poc.py so the upstream honors trusted-proxy identity headers and exposes proxy context for debugging. Verified locally by sending X-Auth-Request-Email and related forwarded headers to /api/me and /api/proxy-context; the app reported auth_source=trusted-proxy and returned the forwarded values correctly.

--- Episode 7 [codex 2026-03-23] (similarity: 0.4529) ---
On 2026-03-23 in fleet-platform-app, targeted Event Grid remediation API failures in prod had two independent causes. First, azr-fleet-platform-prod was missing AUTH_TENANT_ID and AUTH_ALLOWED_AUDIENCES, causing backend function auth to return 'JWT validation is not configured; AUTH_TENANT_ID/AZURE_TENANT_ID missing' and 503 Authentication service unavailable for http_eventgrid_subscription_targeted. Fix: set AUTH_TENANT_ID=570057f4-73ef-41c8-bcbb-08db2fc15c2b and AUTH_ALLOWED_AUDIENCES=api://daf679b9-9134-42a1-9998-9cfac279747f,3eabce94-dd29-4358-b262-09a43f2ddb60 on azr-fleet-platform-prod and update deploy workflows to keep them. Second, the public APIM route still failed even after auth was fixed because agent-fleet-api APIM policy validated only old audience api://agent-fleet-platform and rewrote targeted Event Grid management paths to backend /api/customers/... instead of /api/v1/customers/.... ApiManagementGatewayLogs confirmed 404 backend URL https://azr-fleet-platform-prod.../api/customers/{ddi}/subscriptions/{sub}/eventgrid/subscription for correlation ids dc96056e-e73b-4775-9f1d-7e9c54d0c2cc and 6cd9e891-3f89-4fa1-b3cc-9841e3f39349. Repo fix is to add {{api-audience}} as JWT audience in global-policy*.xml and preserve /v1 for /v1/customers/.../eventgrid/... paths in APIM rewrite logic, plus maintain api-audience named value in deploy workflows.

--- Episode 8 [codex 2026-04-09] (similarity: 0.4478) ---
On 2026-04-09 in azr-compliance-v3, integrated Event Grid remediation on the Event Grid Details page as a browser-side Fleet Platform call rather than a backend proxy because the browser reliably has the operator MSAL Bearer token while the backend, behind APIM, may not retain the raw Authorization credential in deployed environments. The page now uses metadata.coverage.customer_id (aliased from ddi), requests an optional Fleet-specific scope via VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_SCOPE, calls POST /v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription against VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_BASE_URL, polls statusUrl until terminal runtimeStatus, treats succeeded/already_exists as success, maps 404 to not_found, and maps 409 to not_eligible. UI adds per-subscription Ensure Event Grid and Ensure All Deployable actions plus local remediation state badges.

--- Episode 9 [codex 2026-03-25] (similarity: 0.4381) ---
On 2026-03-25, investigated 403s from Rackspace VPN IP 72.32.180.178 to fazure-oauth.mscloud.rackspace.com/register. Live AGW logs showed OPTIONS /register returned 200, while POST /register was blocked by global WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax with rule 931130 (Possible Remote File Inclusion / Off-Domain Reference/Link) on client_uri=https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/inspector and redirect_uris=http://localhost:6274/oauth/callback/debug, followed by 949110 anomaly score block. Updated global WAF custom rules by moving BlockKnownPath from priority 5 to 6 and adding AllowTrustedVpnFazureOauthMcpAuth at priority 5 for Host=fazure-oauth.mscloud.rackspace.com, RequestUri regex ^/(\.well-known/oauth-authorization-server|register|authorize(?:/callback)?|token|jwks|oauth2/authorize-internal)$, and Rackspace VPN IP ranges. This bypasses managed WAF inspection and the generic authorize/token path blocker for trusted internal MCP OAuth registration/auth flows.

--- Episode 10 [codex 2026-03-23] (similarity: 0.4375) ---
On 2026-03-23 in fleet-platform-app, Event Grid eligibility was tightened so Event Grid subscriptions are only created/maintained for monitoring-entitled service blocks. Changes were made locally in source_data, eventgrid activity/orchestrators, onboarding, validation, http_eventgrid, and OpenAPI docs. Customer-wide Event Grid orchestration now uses only Lighthouse subscriptions with computed_service_tier='advanced'; ensure_event_subscription_single now skips non-lighthouse or non-monitoring subscriptions; the targeted Event Grid API returns 409 for subscriptions without monitoring entitlement; onboarding foundation state now marks non-monitoring subscriptions as Event Grid skipped rather than failed. Validation passed with pytest on test_eventgrid_helpers.py and test_http_eventgrid.py plus pre-commit and validate-openapi-spec.
